title: Open Problem (Bravyi et al. [2])
statement_text: Does there exist a $k$-pairable state of size $n = \mathrm{poly}(k)$?
context_text: Bravyi et al. constructed $k$-pairable states of size exponential in $k$, namely $n = 2^{3k}$, using Reed–Muller codes, and left the existence of a $k$-pairable state of size $n = \mathrm{poly}(k)$ as an open problem. This paper addresses this question in the more general setting of $k$-stabilizer universal graph states.
attributed_to: Bravyi, Sharma, Szegedy, and de Wolf
paper_contribution: Provides two explicit constructions of $k$-stabilizer universal graph states (a generalisation of $k$-pairable states) on $n = O(k^{4})$ qubits using the incidence graph of the projective plane over a finite field $\mathbb{F}_{q}$, giving a polynomial improvement over the previously known explicit $n = O(2^{3k})$ construction.
notes: The open problem is stated verbatim in the introduction: 'leaving the existence of a k-pairable states of size n=poly(k) as an open problem'. Attribution is to reference [2] (Bravyi et al., Quantum Information Processing, 2023, arXiv:2211.06497).
paper_title: Vertex-minor universal graphs for generating entangled quantum subsystems
